shower.thought: We spend our whole lives learning how to be human, but nobody hands us the syllabus.
Time moves slower when you're waiting for something and faster when you're dreading it—maybe time isn't real and we're just measuring our own anxiety.
You'll never remember most of the days that mattered, but you'll never forget most of the days that didn't.
The saddest part about getting older isn't losing your youth—it's realizing how much of it you spent worrying about losing it.
Every song you love is technically a recording of the past, so you're never actually listening to music in real time.
You can't taste food when you have a cold, which means flavor might be 80% smell and we're all just smelling soup.
The people who brag about not needing sleep are the same ones who don't realize they're just describing sleep deprivation.
Nostalgia is just grief for a time period instead of a person.
You probably have a more vivid memory of someone from a movie than someone you met in real life, which means fiction is literally more memorable than reality.
If you're always busy, you never have to think about whether you're happy.
The scariest part of social media isn't that people are watching you—it's that you're watching them and believing their highlight reel is their life.
You don't actually know if you're lazy or just intelligent enough to conserve energy.
Every time you remember something, you're not recalling the original memory—you're remembering the last time you remembered it, like a photocopy of a photocopy.
The more successful someone becomes at hiding their problems, the more we believe they don't have any.
You learn what love is supposed to feel like from songs and movies, then spend your life wondering why reality doesn't match the script.
Parallel parking would be impossible to learn if we all learned it the first time we tried.
We say "rest in peace" about death but "good night" about sleep—maybe we're just using the wrong one.
The people who say they "don't have a type" definitely have a type; they just haven't dated enough people to notice it yet.
You've probably forgotten more information than most people will ever learn, which means your brain is choosing what matters instead of what's important.
If you're afraid of being alone, you might actually just be afraid of yourself.

motivational.thought: Comparison is the thief of progress. Focus on your own race. — Unknown
The mind is everything. What you think, you create. — Unknown
Don't wait for the perfect moment. Take the moment and make it perfect. — Unknown
Your biggest regrets won't be the things you did—they'll be the things you didn't try. — Unknown
Small daily improvements compound into remarkable results over time. — James Clear
The obstacle is the way forward, not a sign to stop. — Marcus Aurelius
You are not stuck. You are simply choosing comfort over growth. — Unknown
What lies behind you and what lies before you pale in comparison to what lies within you. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Discipline is choosing what you want most over what you want right now. — Unknown
The person you'll become is determined by the books you read and the people you surround yourself with. — Unknown
Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will. — Unknown
Your past does not determine your future. Only your choices do. — Unknown
The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now. — Ancient Proverb
Progress is progress, no matter how small. Keep moving. — Unknown
You cannot rise above your self-image. Change how you see yourself. — Unknown
Comfort is a slow death for ambition. — Unknown
The work you do on yourself is the most important work you'll ever do. — Unknown
Your effort today is the foundation of your tomorrow. — Unknown
Stop waiting to feel ready. Readiness comes after you start. — Unknown
Resistance is proof you're heading toward something worthwhile. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought: My grandfather said "Your generation is too reliant on technology." I unplugged his life support to prove him wrong.
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.
My therapist asked why I keep sabotaging my relationships. I said, "I don't know, but I'm sure we'll figure it out before you fire me too."
I told my wife she was drawing her eyebrows too high. She looked surprised.
The secret to a long marriage is compromise. My wife wanted a dog, I didn't. We got a dog.
I'm reading a book on the history of glue. Can't put it down.
My dentist told me I need to floss more. I told him I need to see a dentist who minds his own business.
I said to my friend, "I think you're overreacting to your cancer diagnosis." He said, "You're right, I should be grateful it's not lupus."
My kids asked me what's for dinner. I said, "Surprise!" Turns out we're out of food.
I used to think I had insomnia. Turns out I just have a life I'm afraid to fall asleep in.
My mother-in-law visited last week and broke her leg. I said, "What happened?" She said, "I slipped on the ice." I said, "Well, the good news is you have a great excuse to stay longer."
I told my boss I was having trouble concentrating at work. He said, "Sounds like a personal problem." So I made it his personal problem by quitting.
When people ask if I believe in life after death, I say, "I hope not. I'm barely managing this one."
My neighbor asked if I could keep my dog from barking at 3 AM. I said, "I'll try, but honestly, we're both just dealing with insomnia."
I'm not saying my relationship is on the rocks, but my partner suggested we "take some space." I told her we live in a studio apartment, so she took the good news in stride.
My doctor said I drink too much coffee. I said, "Doc, how do you know what I can handle?" He said, "Your heart just texted me asking for a restraining order."
I asked my friend how his divorce was going. He said, "Great, I've never been happier." I said, "That's the spirit," and changed the subject before he realized I'd just insulted his marriage.
I told my kid that money doesn't grow on trees. He said, "Then why are you always saying you're broke?" Clearly, he's going to be a lawyer.
My therapist says I need to set better boundaries. I said, "You're right. Our sessions are over." She said, "That's not what I meant." I said, "Exactly. Boundaries."
I asked my friend if he believes in ghosts. He said, "Not really." I said, "Neither did my last three roommates."
